prose:
Calculation summary (method-provenance block)
• Property: HOMO–LUMO gap of ethylene (Koopmans‐style orbital gap, eV)
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density-fitting (PySCF backend)
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz”); single-point calculation, no further geometry relaxation (notation: DFT//input-xyz).
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Software: chemkit frontier-orbitals skill (PySCF engine).
• Convergence & warnings: SCF converged in 6 cycles; no warnings reported by the engine.
  (integrity check status = “ok”, trustworthy = true).
• Live run log: the calculation streamed to the usual chemkit live “*.out” file in the working directory; the exact CLI used is reproduced below for full reproducibility.

Headline numerical result
HOMO–LUMO gap = **7.4357165 eV**

Additional frontier-orbital details
– HOMO energy = −7.6155818 eV
– LUMO energy = −0.17986527 eV
– Vertical ionization potential (Koopmans) = 7.6155818 eV
– Vertical electron affinity (Koopmans) = 0.17986527 eV

Reproducibility information
CLI invocation (as recorded by chemkit):
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpmlj_ecvp/live.json

The JSON results (path above) contain the full orbital table and energies; the live engine output was written concurrently to the corresponding “frontier_<timestamp>.out” file for inspection or `tail ‑f` monitoring.
